summary: Gruber reviews the Super Bowl LIX commercials, declaring Nike's 'So Win' spot the clear winner. He laments that most Super Bowl ads are offensively bland despite their enormous cost, failing to be memorable or meaningful. Nike's black-and-white celebration of women's sports, featuring Caitlin Clark and narrated by Doechii over Led Zeppelin, struck him as a brand-reinvigorating manifesto comparable to Apple's 'Think Different' campaign. He gives honorable mentions to ChatGPT's technology-history branding spot and Stella Artois's David Beckham twin comedy, while singling out Tubi's grotesque cowboy-hat-head ad as a dishonorable mention.
key_points: Most Super Bowl commercials squander their $8 million slots on forgettable, offensively bland content
Nike's 'So Win' ad succeeded by combining gorgeous cinematography, Led Zeppelin, and a genuine message about women's sports into something that felt like a brand manifesto
Nike hadn't run a Super Bowl ad since 1998, and the spot was likely aimed at reinvigorating employees as much as consumers, drawing parallels to Apple's 'Think Different'
ChatGPT's ad effectively positioned AI alongside historical technological breakthroughs, evoking Apple's WWDC 2013 opening film
The measure of a great Super Bowl ad is simple: is it memorable, and does it actually make you feel something?
money_quotes: The problem with modern Super Bowl commercials is they're bland. Offensively bland.

The commercial time is famously expensive — Fox was supposedly selling 30-second spots for last night's game for $8 million — but the sponsors who buy that time tend to squander it with absolute mindless pap.
Fewer and fewer sponsors each year, it seems, attempt to bring something actually interesting. Something different. Something with some punch.
One simple measure is whether the ads are memorable. Most of the ads in last night's game I forgot by the time the game resumed after the break.
I don't know why anyone would book and commission a Super Bowl commercial if they weren't trying to do something or say something memorable with the spot.
Nike hadn't run a Super Bowl ad since 1998, and they came back with a banger that to me reinvigorated the Nike brand.
The message I took was that Tubi is the streaming app that will make you feel grossed out.
key_insight: Nike's 'So Win' ad stood out from the Super Bowl's sea of expensive blandness by actually having something to say — a genuine manifesto about women's sports that reinvigorated the brand.
